secret-isolation.description: Provider-bridge and cross-surface secret isolation (ADR-044). Credential-shaped values captured at the AI SDK provider bridge's request/response capture seam (`bridgeExtras`: raw requestBody, response headers, warnings) and general runtime surfaces (persisted kernel records, captured canonical stream events, captured telemetry, and recorded transcripts) must never carry raw secret material. KRT-BD003 declared the reusable secret-isolation fixture (spec/conformance/providers/fixtures/secret-isolation-fixtures.json) staging representative, non-real provider/MCP credentials; KRT-BD004 promoted the value-equality `secretAbsence` check set (scanning for the fixture's configured `secretValues` plus their common encoded/derived leak-form variants) across the runtime-api, telemetry, and MCP-client surfaces (spec/conformance/engine/plans/runtime-api-callables-extended.json, spec/conformance/telemetry/plans/framework-operational-telemetry.json, spec/conformance/tools/plans/providers-mcp-client.json). KRT-BK004 closed a gap at the AI SDK provider bridge seam itself: `bridgeExtras` (raw captured requestBody/response headers/warnings) previously passed only through JSON-safety normalization (`sanitizeMetadataValue`) with no pattern-based secret detection before entering `buildProviderMetadata`'s `aiSdkBridge` field, which backs both `tool_call.done`-shaped stream chunks and the metadata that ultimately reaches durable run records. The shared structural secret-pattern primitives (secret-key-shaped names, URL-embedded credentials, connection strings, auth headers, credential assignments, JWTs, long-secretish tokens) were relocated to `@tuvren/core/security` so both `@tuvren/runtime`'s telemetry-attribute screen and the provider bridge can apply the identical pattern set; `screenValueForSecretPatterns` now screens `bridgeExtras` only (never `providerMetadata`, which legitimately carries long opaque reasoning-signature strings such as `anthropic.signature`/`google.thoughtSignature`/`vertex.thoughtSignature`) before it reaches `aiSdkBridge`. The new `secretPatternAbsence` assertion kind — structural, detecting secret-*shaped* values via that same shared pattern set rather than value-equality against a configured list — covers the provider-bridge seam alongside the pre-existing value-equality `secretAbsence` check in the new `secret-isolation.provider-bridge-request-and-headers` check (spec/conformance/telemetry/plans/framework-operational-telemetry.json), exercising the bridge directly at the `TuvrenProvider` generate/stream level.
